Class FlatFileGenerator

java.lang.Object
io.bloviate.file.FlatFileGenerator
All Implemented Interfaces:
FileGenerator
public class FlatFileGenerator extends Object implements FileGenerator
Generator for creating flat files (CSV, TSV, pipe-delimited) with synthetic data.

The FlatFileGenerator creates delimited text files containing randomly generated data based on configurable column definitions. It supports multiple output formats including CSV, tab-delimited (TSV), and pipe-delimited files.

Key features:

  • Support for CSV, TSV, and pipe-delimited output formats
  • Configurable number of rows and columns
  • Automatic header generation with column names
  • Integration with ColumnDefinition and DataGenerator
  • YAML configuration export capability

Example usage: 

List<ColumnDefinition> columns = Arrays.asList(
    new ColumnDefinition("id", new IntegerGenerator(random)),
    new ColumnDefinition("name", new StringGenerator(random))
);

new FlatFileGenerator.Builder("output-file")
    .addAll(columns)
    .rows(1000)
    .output(new CsvFile())
    .build()
    .generate();
Author:
Tim Veil
See Also:
  • ColumnDefinition
  • FileDefinition
  • FileGenerator

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static class 
    FlatFileGenerator.Builder
    Builds a FlatFileGenerator, defaulting to CSV output and 1000 rows with no columns.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    generate()
    Generates the output data file, writing one header row followed by the configured number of data rows.
    List<ColumnDefinition>
    getColumnDefinitions()
    Returns the column definitions, in output order.
    FileDefinition
    getDefinition()
    Returns the file format definition controlling the delimiter and extension.
    String
    getFileName()
    Returns the base output file name (the format-specific extension is appended at generation time).
    long
    getRows()
    Returns the number of data rows to generate, excluding the header row.
    void
    yaml()
    Exports this generator's configuration to a YAML file (named after the generator's base file name with a .yaml extension).
